summary.text: Provides extension methods for adding GitHub Models resources to the application model.

members.name: AddGitHubModel
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<GitHubModelResource> GitHubModelsExtensions.AddGitHubModel(this IDistributedApplicationBuilder builder, string name, string model, IResourceBuilder<ParameterResource>? organization = null)

summary.text: Adds a GitHub Model resource to the application model.

model.text: The model name to use with GitHub Models.

name.text: The name of the resource. This name will be used as the connection string name when referenced in a dependency.

organization.text: The organization login associated with the organization to which the request is to be attributed.

members.name: AddGitHubModel
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<GitHubModelResource> GitHubModelsExtensions.AddGitHubModel(this IDistributedApplicationBuilder builder, string name, GitHubModel model, IResourceBuilder<ParameterResource>? organization = null)

summary.text: Adds a GitHub Model resource to the application model using a

children.text: This overload is not available in polyglot app hosts. Use the string-based overload instead.

remarks.text: Create a GitHub Model resource for the Microsoft Phi-3 Medium Instruct model:

name.text: The name of the resource. This name will be used as the connection string name when referenced in a dependency.

members.name: WithApiKey
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<GitHubModelResource> GitHubModelsExtensions.WithApiKey(this IResourceBuilder<GitHubModelResource> builder, IResourceBuilder<ParameterResource> apiKey)

summary.text: Configures the API key for the GitHub Model resource from a parameter.

description.text: Thrown when the provided parameter is not marked as secret.

summary.text: Adds a health check to the GitHub Model resource.

children.text: This method adds a health check that verifies the GitHub Models endpoint is accessible, the API key is valid, and the specified model is available. The health check will:

description.text: with details when the API key is invalid (HTTP 401)
with error details when the model is unknown (HTTP 404)

children.text: Because health checks are included in the rate limit of the GitHub Models API, it is recommended to use this health check sparingly, such as when you are having issues understanding the reason the model is not working as expected. Furthermore, the health check will run a single time per application instance.

Models published by DeepSeek.
DeepSeek-R1 excels at reasoning tasks using a step-by-step training process, such as language, scientific reasoning, and coding tasks.

DeepSeek-V3-0324 demonstrates notable improvements over its predecessor, DeepSeek-V3, in several key aspects, including enhanced reasoning, improved function calling, and superior code generation capabilities.

remarks.text: If set, the token must be attributed to an organization.
